zh.Alnwick Castle, Northumberland, England,: 英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德阿恩維克 卡素
zh.lifeSummary: Gilbert de Tesson Lord of Alnwick是Raoul II de La Roche-Tesson的兒子，於1035年出生在法國諾門迪可騰。他和Beatrix of Normandy至少有2個兒子。他去世於1066年在法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯享年31歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Tesson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Arnaud, Elmire, Gaston, Pierre.
French (mainly Vendée and Normandy): from tesson, a dialect word meaning ‘badger’, applied as a nickname for someone thought to resemble the animal in some way.
Altered form of French   Tison  . Compare   Teson  .

variants.name: Tison
meanings: French: nickname from Old French tison ‘piece of wood’ and ‘firebrand’, in the latter sense perhaps denoting someone with a fiery temperament.
French (Nord and Pas-de-Calais): ethnic name from a derivative of Old French teis ‘German, Flemish’, from ancient Germanic theod ‘people’. Compare Teson and Tesson .
Possibly also an Americanized form of German   Theissen   or   Theisen  . Compare   Tyson  .

nameMeanings.name: Gilbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from gisil ‘pledge’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. This was the name of the founder of the only native British religious order (abolished at the Dissolution of the Monasteries), St Gilbert of Sempringham ( ?1083–1189 ), in whose honour it is still sometimes bestowed, especially among Roman Catholics. It gained a wider currency in the 19th century.

en.lifeSummary: Gilbert de Tesson Lord of Alnwick was born in 1035, in Cotentin, Normandy, France as the son of Raoul II de La Roche-Tesson. He had at least 2 sons with Beatrix of Normandy. He died in 1066, in Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, at the age of 31.

ja.lifeSummary: Gilbert de Tesson Lord of Alnwickは1035年にフランスノーマンディコテンティンでRaoul II de La Roche-Tessonの息子として生まれた。彼はBeatrix of Normandyとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼は1066年にフランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスで31歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Gilbert de Tesson Lord of Alnwick(은)는 1035년 프랑스 노먼디 컷틴에서 Raoul II de La Roche-Tesson의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Beatrix of Normandy(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1066년 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이에서 31세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Gilbert de Tesson Lord of Alnwick родился в 1035 г. в местности котентинь, норманди, Франция. Его отца звали Raoul II de La Roche-Tesson. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына от Beatrix of Normandy. Он умер в 1066 г. в местности норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция в возрасте 31 год.
